name: Grilled Balsamic Veal Chops
recipeInstructions:
To be served with Balsamic Ratatouille and Mashed Potatoes with Mascarpone Cheese & Roasted Garlic
1. Roughly chop shallots, garlic, and rosemary. Place them in a casserole dish large enough to hold the veal chops, along with 2 cups of balsamic vinegar and olive oil. Add salt and pepper. Marinate the veal chops in the refrigerator for 6 to 8 hours.
2. Remove the veal chops from the marinade. Place them on a hot grill. Cook over medium temperature, turning once, for 9 to 13 minutes, until cooked to your liking. For gas grills, use the indirect method until chops reach 160°F for medium and 170°F for well-done.
3. Reduce the remaining 1 cup of balsamic vinegar in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring frequently, until thick and syrupy, about 8 to 10 minutes. Do not let the vinegar boil rapidly or it will burn. You will get approximately 4 teaspoons.
4. To assemble: On a warm plate, place 1/4 of the ratatouille in the center. Place 1/4 of the mashed potatoes on top of each serving of ratatouille. Drizzle 1 teaspoon of basil oil over each serving of potatoes. Lay the grilled chops over the potatoes and drizzle 1 teaspoon of reduced balsamic vinegar over each. Garnish with thyme sprigs.
